summary: Flysurfer's 3-strut open-C LEI kite with a 3-stage adjustable bridle: set it to Wave mode for more depower and bar force, Freeride mode for kiteloops and stability, or Freestyle mode for explosive pop and slack. Explosive, responsive, and deeply versatile.
best_for: Riders who want one kite that does freestyle, waves, and freeride

sources.summary: The 3-stage bridle system is genuinely useful. Switching between wave and freestyle mode makes a real difference. Great open-C feel at a Flysurfer price point.
Underrated kite. The open-C pop is real and it waves better than you'd expect. Good option for riders who switch between sessions.

structured_review.summary: The Flysurfer Stoke (reviewed as v2, now in 2025 third iteration) is a lively, fast-turning 3-strut open-C kite with a clever adjustable bridle system targeting wave and freestyle riders. Bar pressure sits in a comfortable middle ground — not too light, not too heavy — and upwind performance drew genuine praise. The main caveat from reviewers is that it feels noticeably different from traditional Flysurfer foil DNA, and the light steering feel can cause the bar to swap around unexpectedly in lighter winds.
pros: Fast, playful turns with responsive steering
Bar pressure well-balanced — not too light, not too heavy
Strong upwind performance noted by multiple riders
Comfortable, durable-feeling bar with adjustable width
SUP valve built-in with adapter stored in front strut
Non-snag tip pads and adjustable bridle system are clever details
cons: Light steering feel can cause bar to swap unexpectedly
Feels very different from traditional Flysurfer foil character — may disappoint loyal Flysurfer fans
Drift ability in waves not fully tested in this review
Limited transcript depth on 3-stage bridle performance differences
structured_review.best_for: Intermediate to advanced riders who want one kite covering waves, freestyle, and freeride with an adjustable feel.
structured_review.not_for: Flysurfer foil loyalists expecting familiar handling, or beginners who need predictable, forgiving bar response.
structured_review.rec_blurb: A chameleon open-C that dials in wave, freeride, or freestyle mode via one bridle adjustment.
